question: A webhook boundary event template rejects the value `__order_paid__` in its Webhook ID field. Which validation constraint on the `inbound.context` property explains the rejection?

options.text: It must be a fully-qualified URL matching `^https?://.*`.
Any characters are allowed, but the value must be valid JSON under 256 bytes.
It must match `^[a-zA-Z0-9]+([-_][a-zA-Z0-9]+)*$` — letters, numbers, single underscores/hyphens, and may not begin or end with an underscore/hyphen.
It must be exactly 16 hexadecimal characters generated by the modeler.

a.text: The `^https?://.*` pattern is the REST Endpoint URL constraint on the outbound REST template, not the Webhook ID of the webhook template.

b.text: The Webhook ID property is not JSON-typed; its constraint is a regex on the value itself, not a byte-length or JSON validity check.

c.text: The webhook boundary event template's `inbound.context` declares `constraints.pattern.value: "^[a-zA-Z0-9]+([-_][a-zA-Z0-9]+)*$"` with the message that it can only contain letters, numbers, or single underscores/hyphens and cannot begin or end with an underscore/hyphen. `__order_paid__` starts and ends with an underscore and contains a run of two, so it fails.

d.text: There is no fixed-length hex constraint on the Webhook ID; the value is user-supplied and validated against the documented regex.

explanation: The webhook boundary event template constrains the `inbound.context` (Webhook ID) value with the regex `^[a-zA-Z0-9]+([-_][a-zA-Z0-9]+)*$` and the message that it can only contain letters, numbers, or single underscores and hyphens, and cannot begin or end with an underscore or hyphen. `__order_paid__` violates the rule three ways — it starts with `_`, ends with `_`, and contains consecutive `_` — so the modeler rejects it.
